scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-researched against Tavus's current OpenAPI spec (docs.tavus.io/openapi.yaml): the legacy `replicas`/`personas` naming has been renamed to `faces`/`PALs` account-wide, with `/v2/replicas`, `replica_id`, `/v2/personas`, and `persona_id` all still documented as supported LEGACY ALIASES for the new `faces`/`pal_id` shapes - this bundle's `replicas` stream keeps the wave2 legacy-parity path unchanged since it is still a fully-supported, non-deprecated alias. 10 read streams (replicas/faces, videos, conversations, pals, guardrails, objectives, documents, pronunciation_dictionaries, voices, skills) and 15 write actions covering video/conversation/pal/guardrail/objective/document/pronunciation-dictionary create-delete plus end_conversation. Excluded: lipsync and replacements (both endpoint groups are marked `deprecated: true` - Tavus no longer supports them); canvas interactions, deployments, tools, pal-skills/pal-tools attachment sub-resources, transcriptions, and the pals/check-username utility endpoint as out-of-scope breadth-vs-cost triage; every rename/patch (JSON Patch RFC 6902 body) endpoint as an ENGINE_GAP (this dialect's body_type has no JSON-Patch-array shape, only a flat JSON object/form/none).

the CURRENT-naming equivalent of the covered replicas stream; /v2/replicas is documented as a still-supported, non-deprecated legacy alias returning the identical record shape, so modeling both would duplicate the same resource under two paths

trains a new face from video/image footage; an asynchronous, billed, rights-sensitive operation (requires the caller to attest to likeness/footage permissions) not modeled as a plain reverse-ETL create action this pass
single-face detail; identical record shape to the replicas list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
irreversibly deletes a face (optionally hard-deleting training footage); the response's own docs flag hard=true as irrevocable
face rename; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, low-value single-field rename not covered as a write action this pass
single-video detail; identical record shape to the videos list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
video rename; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, delete_video already covers the primary destructive-lifecycle write for this resource
single-conversation detail; identical record shape to the conversations list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
records a Magic Canvas interaction on an ACTIVE live conversation - a real-time interaction-protocol call, not an at-rest object mutation; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
